channel.title: BeastsNetwork | Aggregated Cybersecurity News
channel.description: Real-time cybersecurity news aggregated from top sources including The Hacker News, BleepingComputer, SANS ISC, ESET, Exploit-DB, and more.

item.title: CISA Warns of Active Exploitation of Critical VMware Bug
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has warned of active exploitation of a critical vulnerability in VMware products.
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Critical Vulnerability in Popular Cloud Platform Fixed
item.description: A critical vulnerability in a widely used cloud platform has been patched. Users are advised to update immediately to prevent potential exploitation.
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup
item.description: The FBI and CISA have updated their March warning about Russian intelligence phishing Signal accounts, and the operators have added a step: they now coax targets into handing over their Signal Backup Recovery Key.
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Phishing Kit Targets Cloud Service Providers
item.description: Threat actors are deploying new phishing kits specifically targeting cloud service providers to gain unauthorized access to cloud infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Phishing Kit Bypasses Multi-Factor Authentication
item.description: A new phishing kit has been discovered that can bypass multi-factor authentication, putting even well-protected accounts at risk.
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Ransomware Group Uses Double Extortion Tactics
item.description: A newly emerged ransomware group is employing double extortion tactics, encrypting victim data and threatening to leak it if ransom demands are not met.
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New SharkLoader Malware Deploys Cobalt Strike
item.description: A new variant of SharkLoader malware has been observed deploying Cobalt Strike beacons as part of its attack chain, targeting organizations worldwide.
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
item.description: The Security Service of Ukraine (SSU) said it, together with the U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), uncovered a long-running campaign orchestrated by Russian intelligence services to break into the messaging accounts of government officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists in Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OpenAI Previews GPT-5.6 Sol With Restricted Access and Stronger Cyber Safeguards
item.description: OpenAI on Friday released three versions of GPT-5.6, called Sol, Terra, and Luna, as a limited preview to a small number of companies as part of an ongoing engagement with the U.S. government.
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: FBI Warns Russian Intelligence Hackers Target Signal Backup Recovery Keys
item.description: The FBI and CISA have updated their March warning about Russian intelligence phishing Signal accounts, and the operators have added a step: they now coax targets into handing over their Signal Backup Recovery Key.
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Clean GitHub repo tricks AI coding agents into running malware
item.description: An agentic coding tool tasked with cloning and setting up a seemingly benign GitHub repository could execute a malicious payload that remains invisible to security scanners, AI agents, and human reviewers.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: FBI: Russian hackers now target Signal backup recovery keys
item.description: The FBI and CISA are warning that a phishing campaign targeting Signal users tied to Russian intelligence services has evolved to steal Signal Backup Recovery Keys, allowing attackers to access victims' historical messages.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: CISA sets urgent deadline to fix Cisco flaw exploited in attacks
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is giving federal agencies until Sunday to patch a vulnerability in Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server that is being actively exploited.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Polymarket customers lose $3 million in supply-chain attack
item.description: Polymarket says it will fully reimburse customers who lost an estimated $3 million after hackers injected a malicious script into the platform's frontend following a breach at a third-party vendor.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Cybersecurity firms targeted by fraudulent OpenAI organization invites
item.description: Threat actors are creating OpenAI tenants that impersonate legitimate companies and inviting employees to join them, in what appears to be a ploy to trick targets into submitting sensitive company information in chats and projects.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Your First GRC Agent: A Red Teamer's Walkthrough
item.description: AI won't replace GRC analysts, but it can eliminate much of the repetitive work they do. Anecdotes walks through building an agent that continuously monitors controls, identifies evidence gaps, and opens remediation tasks.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Anthropic is testing desktop-like Claude Cowork for mobile
item.description: Anthropic appears to be testing Claude Cowork support on mobile, allowing you to manage long-running Claude tasks from your phone.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Poland busts SIM-swapping gang tied to millions in crypto theft
item.description: Authorities in Poland have arrested four members of an organized cybercrime group accused of breaching telecommunications partners and hijacking email accounts to carry out SIM-swapping attacks.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Order-tracking app Shop abused to push callback phishing attacks
item.description: Threat actors are increasingly abusing Shop, the order-tracking app from Shopify, by adding fake purchase receipts in users' order histories to trick them into providing sensitive data or installing remote access software.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Microsoft quietly extends free Windows 10 ESU support to October 2027
item.description: Microsoft has quietly extended its free Windows 10 Extended Security Updates (ESU) program for consumers by an additional year, allowing enrolled devices to continue receiving security updates until October 12, 2027.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: New macOS malware embeds fake errors to confuse AI analysis tools
item.description: A newly discovered macOS malware dubbed "Gaslight" is designed to confuse AI-assisted malware analysis tools by hiding prompt injection strings and fake debugging data within the executable.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: PirloTV sports piracy network disrupted as 44 domains seized
item.description: A major sports piracy ring linked to the illegal PirloTV streaming platform has been disrupted in an action that targeted 44 domains.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Bluekit phishing kit adopts browser-in-the-middle for login theft
item.description: The Bluekit phishing-as-a-service platform continues to evolve with nearly 70 new hostnames identified over the past week and by adding browser-in-the-middle capabilities for improved data theft.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: The Four Elevations of Effective Fraud Prevention
item.description: Fraudsters don't attack just one transaction. They target accounts, platforms, and entire ecosystems. IPQS explains the four elevations of fraud prevention and why broader visibility improves fraud detection.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Webinar: Why account takeovers remain one of the hardest threats to stop
item.description: Account takeover attacks continue to challenge security teams because attackers often operate through legitimate accounts and trusted services. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help organizations identify compromised accounts faster and automate response workflows.
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: What do Ports Hear When Nobody's Listening? An Assessment of Automated Cybercrime
item.description: Guest diary by Nicole Phillips discussing automated cybercrime, botnets, and the layers of orchestration in the automated shadow economy.
item.dc:creator: SANS ISC

item.title: Linux Process Name Masquerading
item.description: Discussion about stack strings with a practical example. Interest in malware obfuscation techniques, specifically looking at process names and how malicious processes can mimic non-suspicious names.
item.dc:creator: SANS ISC
